steps.title: Recognize the plural noun
steps.content: The sentence introduces the judges for the 1974 Nobel Prize in Literature—plural—as the entity making the decision. The subject pronoun in the blank must be plural to maintain pronoun-antecedent agreement.

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: pronoun-antecedent agreement. Rule: a subject pronoun must match its antecedent in number.
  • Choice A — "you": second-person pronoun; does not refer to "the judges" at all. ✗
  • Choice B — "it": singular pronoun; disagrees with plural "the judges." ✗
  • Choice C — "anyone": singular indefinite pronoun; disagrees with plural antecedent. ✗
  • Choice D — "they": plural pronoun; agrees with plural "the judges." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
